channel.title: Crime-Justice on The Daily Thrum
channel.description: Recent content in Crime-Justice on The Daily Thrum

item.title: Birmingham Assailants Identified As Two Women Dressed As Police Officers
item.description:
As Birmingham Police reportedly began to carry out an investigation to identify people involved in an assault of a white male in Birmingham city centre after footage of the incident went viral on X, citizen journalists issued an update confirming that two of the assailants had now been identified as two women dressed as police officers.

The update followed widespread public confusion after footage appeared to show women in hats and hi-vis vests intervening in an incident, holding the man being assaulted only after he complied, having apparently mistaken the women for actual officers. Several participants from a community requiring sensitive policing were allowed to leave the area in a manner consistent with modern British policing.

item.title: Police Arrest Man Being Assaulted After Determining He Was Closest And Least Administratively Challenging
item.description:
BIRMINGHAM, UK — Footage has circulated online showing a man being repeatedly struck by a group of individuals while police officers stand nearby, carefully assessing the situation before concluding that the most efficient course of action was to arrest the person currently being hit while allowing several other men involved in the confrontation to leave in the traditional manner of people assessed as inconvenient to pursue.

At one stage, the man appears to lash out at one of the officers, but as the adrenaline fades he seems to recognise they are police, after which he quickly settles down.

item.title: Former Special Operator Who Neutralised Eight Home Invaders Convicted After Failing To Wait Until Shot Politely Enough
item.description:
BRISBANE — A former special operator has been convicted of second-degree murder after defending his family from eight armed home invaders in what prosecutors described as “an excessive and worryingly competent response to a challenging interpersonal incident”.

The court heard that Damien Redfern, 42, a former special forces soldier, defensive tactics instructor, private security consultant, emergency-response trainer, and the only man in southern Queensland known to have reverse-engineered a household mop into a non-lethal perimeter deterrent, responded to the break-in “too quickly, too decisively, and with insufficient visible helplessness”.

item.title: Victorian Government Confirms Machete Ban Remains Effective Against Citizens Already Unlikely To Bring A Machete To A Fight
item.description:
MELBOURNE — The Victorian Government has confirmed its machete ban remains highly effective against all citizens who were already unlikely to bring a machete to a fight, despite reports that more than 40 public knife fights have occurred in the year since the policy was announced.

Officials defended the ban after critics described it as an “absolute failure”, saying the policy had successfully prevented thousands of accountants, nurses, retirees, office workers, parents, librarians and people waiting quietly for trams from carrying machetes in public.
